commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Philip, and Bartholomew; Thomas, and Matthew the tax collector; James the son of Alphaeus, and Lebbaeus, whose surname was Thaddaeus; James, the son of Zebedee, called James the greater, put to death by Herod. ) He was brother to John the Evangelist. The other James was called the less, also James of Alpheus, and the brother of the Lord, bishop of Jerusalem, martyred there about the year 61. (Witham) Some take Bartholomew to be the same as Nathaniel. Bartholomew signifies son of Tholmew; and he might have been called Nathaniel, son of Thalmew. (Bible de Vence).

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content:
After this, when he had said, Philip, and Bartholomew, he added, Thomas, and Matthew the Publican. Matthew 10:3

But Luke not so, but in the opposite order, and he puts him before Thomas.

Next, James the son of Alphæus. For there was, as I have already said, the son of Zebedee also.

methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
